preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 62% to win, leaving South Africa a 13% chance of victory. The underdog path is narrow but not implausible; a draw sits at 26%, indicating genuine competitive uncertainty despite Korea's clear edge.

The win-loss split shows Korea materially favored, yet the draw probability near one-quarter suggests the match has a realistic chance of remaining level through regulation. South Africa's modest win odds reflect an underdog position, though not one of overwhelming dominance by Korea. The gap between a Korea win and the combined alternatives (South Africa win plus draw at 39%) is substantial but the competition does not appear predetermined.

Model and market are closely aligned. The sportsbook consensus puts Korea at 59%, only 3 percentage points below the model's 62%, with the draw nearly identical at 24% versus 26%. Polymarket prices track similarly. All three sources cluster within a tight band, suggesting broad agreement on Korea's advantage and the match's overall competitiveness.
